response.justification: Bogdan is an experienced software engineer (20+ years) with strong full-stack and low-latency systems expertise, meeting educational and general experience requirements. However, the role demands specialized expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization that is entirely absent from his resume. His background is in application-layer and infrastructure software, not hardware performance architecture for AI accelerators.
strengths: 20+ years of software engineering with proven ability to optimize performance-critical systems (low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby, high-performance DLang applications)
Strong fundamentals in multiple languages and system layers including C/C++ (National Instruments), DevOps, Docker/Kubernetes, and current involvement in AI-agent infrastructure
Demonstrated ability to learn complex domains and teach others through workshops on web internals, testing, and advanced software engineering practices
concerns: No evidence of SoC design, IP modeling, or experience with hardware description languages (Verilog, VHDL) — a required core skill

No demonstrated expertise in deep neural network optimization, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator performance analysis despite current AI-agent work being infrastructure-focused, not hardware-optimization focused

Career history shows application and infrastructure software specialization, not the hardware-software co-design and chip-level performance analysis required for Principal-level work in AI accelerator architecture

key_factors.factor: No deep learning workload optimization background
Strong foundational software engineering and learning ability
